runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Rust", "build_system": "cargo", "package_manager": "cargo", "build_commands": ["cargo build --workspace --release"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update -y", "apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config libssl-dev nodejs npm", "rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "1.94.0", "system_packages": []}, "repo_type": "desktop_app", "base_image": "rust:1.94.0-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Official Rust image with cargo pre-installed based on Debian bookworm, suitable for building the workspace including the tauri-based desktop app.", "pitfalls": ["Building a tauri-based desktop app inside a container requires Node tooling and frontend assets; ensure frontend is built or prebuilt.", "Running the final image in headless CI may fail due to GUI dependencies; consider separate runtime image with GTK libs."], "notes": "Workspace contains 'core' and 'Flying Carpet/src-tauri' crates. Build uses cargo in a workspace; frontend is a tauri app; production image should be multi-stage to reduce size; you may need to install additional OS dependencies for GTK on Linux and Windows cross-compilation not supported by default."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# Multi-stage build for FlyingCarpet (TAURI) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy source and build the workspace in release mode
COPY . .
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Collect release artifacts into a known directory
RUN mkdir -p /workspace/release
RUN sh -lc 'for f in /workspace/target/release/*; do if [ -x "$f" ]; then cp "$f" "/workspace/release/"; fi; done'


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebKit TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates libssl3 && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the release artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/release /workspace/release

# Wrapper script to execute the released binary
RUN printf '#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nBIN_DIR="/workspace/release"\nfor f in "$BIN_DIR"/*; do\n  if [ -x "$f" ]; then\n    exec "$f" "$@"\n  fi\ndone\nexit 1\n' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh

# Run the app (wrapper will pick the executable in the release dir)
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Stage 1: Builder
FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

WORKDIR /workspace

# Install build dependencies (Rust, Node for frontend assets, etc.)
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        curl \
        ca-certificates \
        build-essential \
        pkg-config \
        libssl-dev \
        nodejs \
        npm \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the entire repository into the build context
COPY . .

# Build the workspace from source (release for production)
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Install runtime dependencies for GUI apps (GTK/WebKit) and certificates
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgtk-3-0 \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0 \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built binary from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /app/FlyingCarpet

# Run the GUI application
CMD ["./FlyingCarpet"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "# Ignore VCS
.git
.gitignore

# Node and frontend artifacts
node_modules/
frontend/node_modules/

# Build outputs
target/
**/*.rs.bk
Cargo.lock

# Misc
*.log
.env
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Stage 1: Builder
FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

WORKDIR /workspace

# Install build dependencies (Rust, Node for frontend assets, etc.)
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        curl \
        ca-certificates \
        build-essential \
        pkg-config \
        libssl-dev \
        libgtk-3-dev \
        nodejs \
        npm \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the entire repository into the build context
COPY . .

# Build the workspace from source (release for production)
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Install runtime dependencies for GUI apps (GTK/WebKit) and certificates
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgtk-3-0 \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0 \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built binary from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /app/FlyingCarpet

# Run the GUI application
CMD ["./FlyingCarpet"]
"}

Consider adding a non-GUI test or enabling virtual display (e.g., xvfb) if you plan to run the app in CI.", "Copying the entire repository into the builder stage can be expanded to only copy necessary files (or use a .dockerignore) to reduce build context size and improve cache efficiency.", "The final binary name FlyingCarpet is assumed; if the Cargo workspace changes binary names, the COPY --from=builder path may break. Consider making the binary name configurable or adding a build-arg for the exact output path."]

  - The build fails because libsoup-3.0 is not installed in the builder image, and the libsoup-3.0.pc pkg-config file is missing. Installing libsoup-3.0 (and its dev package) or providing the correct PKG_CONFIG_PATH should resolve the error.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Stage 1: Builder
FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

WORKDIR /workspace

# Install build dependencies (Rust, Node for frontend assets, etc.)
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        curl \
        ca-certificates \
        build-essential \
        pkg-config \
        libssl-dev \
        libsoup-3-1 \
        libsoup-3-dev \
        libgtk-3-dev \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0-dev \
        nodejs \
        npm \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Ensure pkg-config can find GTK files
ENV PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/pkgconfig:/usr/share/pkgconfig

# Copy the entire repository into the build context
COPY . .

# Build the workspace from source (release for production)
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Install runtime dependencies for GUI apps (GTK/WebKit) and certificates
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgtk-3-0 \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0 \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built binary from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /app/FlyingCarpet

# Run the GUI application
CMD ["./FlyingCarpet"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Stage 1: Builder
FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

WORKDIR /workspace

# Install build dependencies (Rust, Node for frontend assets, etc.)
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        curl \
        ca-certificates \
        build-essential \
        pkg-config \
        libssl-dev \
        libgtk-3-dev \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0-dev \
        nodejs \
        npm \
        libsoup3 \
        libsoup3-dev \
        libsoup-3-1 \
        libsoup-3-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Ensure pkg-config can find GTK files
ENV PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/pkgconfig:/usr/share/pkgconfig

# Copy the entire repository into the build context
COPY . .

# Build the workspace from source (release for production)
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Install runtime dependencies for GUI apps (GTK/WebKit) and certificates
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgtk-3-0 \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0 \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built binary from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /app/FlyingCarpet

# Run the GUI application
CMD ["./FlyingCarpet"]
"}

steps.tool_input: {"query": "libsoup-3-dev Debian Bookworm package name libsoup-3.0 - apt"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Stage 1: Builder
FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

WORKDIR /workspace

# Install build dependencies (Rust, Node for frontend assets, etc.)
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        curl \
        ca-certificates \
        build-essential \
        pkg-config \
        libssl-dev \
        libgtk-3-dev \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0-dev \
        nodejs \
        npm \
        libsoup2.4-dev \
        libsoup2.4-1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Ensure pkg-config can find GTK files
ENV PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/pkgconfig:/usr/share/pkgconfig

# Copy the entire repository into the build context
COPY . .

# Build the workspace from source (release for production)
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Install runtime dependencies for GUI apps (GTK/WebKit) and certificates
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgtk-3-0 \
        libwebkit2gtk-4.0 \
        ca-certificates \
        libsoup2.4-1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built binary from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /app/FlyingCarpet

# Run the GUI application
CMD ["./FlyingCarpet"]
"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Initial failure – missing `gdk-3.0`**  
   - **What happened:** Build failed during `cargo build` for `gdk-sys v0.18.2` with the error *“The system library `gdk-3.0`”* missing.  
   - **Cause:** GTK development libraries were not installed in the builder stage.  
   - **Fix:** Install GTK 3 dev packages:  
     ```bash
     apt-get install -y libgtk-3-dev
     ```

2. **Second failure – missing `libsoup-3.0`**  
   - **What happened:** `cargo build` failed again, now complaining about missing `libsoup-3.0` (for a crate depending on `webkit2gtk` or similar).  
   - **Cause:** The `libsoup-3.0` headers were not available.  
   - **Fix:** Install the correct Debian Bookworm package:  
     ```bash
     apt-get install -y libsoup-3.0-dev
     ```
     (Debian 12 uses `libsoup-3.0-dev`, not `libsoup-3-dev` or `libsoup3-dev`.)

3. **Rebuild attempts failed repeatedly**  
   - The Dockerfile updates were small, but verification kept failing because the core missing dependency (`libsoup-3.0-dev`) was never found/added.  
   - **Fix:** Consolidate the `apt-get install` step to include *all* required native dependencies at once:  
     ```bash
     apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
       pkg-config libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev \
       build-essential curl git ca-certificates && \
       rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
     ```

4. **Runtime concerns – missing GUI libraries and no display server**  
   - **What happened:** Later verify steps mentioned GUI app may fail in headless environments (no X11/Wayland, no Xvfb).  
   - **Fix:** Decide whether to run GUI headlessly:  
     - If only building: no display needed.  
     - If running GUI in CI/container: add a virtual display (`xvfb-run`) or use a base that supports GUI.  

5. **Binary name and COPY mismatch**  
   - **What happened:** Verification hinted that `FlyingCarpet` binary name may not match actual crate output.  
   - **Fix:** After build, verify binary name via `cargo metadata` or inspect `target/release/` and adjust `COPY` + `CMD` accordingly (e.g., `COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/<binary> /usr/local/bin/<binary>`).

6. **Image hygiene and .dockerignore**  
   - **What happened:** One verify step noted missing `.dockerignore` in later attempts.  
   - **Fix:** Keep `.dockerignore` including `/target/`, `node_modules/`, `.git`, etc., to minimize build context.

7. **Final guidance for next attempt:**  
   - Start from `rust:1.94.0-bookworm`.  
   - Install required dev libraries (`libgtk-3-dev`, `libsoup-3.0-dev`, `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev`).  
   - Clean up apt cache.  
   - Build in `/workspace`.  
   - Use a multi-stage build with a minimal runtime image if GUI runs outside container.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the repository and build in release mode
COPY . .

# Build all workspace crates in release mode
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebView TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3-0 ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the built binary from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet

# Run the app
CMD ["FlyingCarpet"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "target/
node_modules/
.dockerignore
.git/
.gitignore
**/*.log
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the repository and build in release mode
COPY . .

# Build all workspace crates in release mode
RUN cargo build --workspace --release


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebView TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the built binary from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet

# Run the app
CMD ["FlyingCarpet"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the repository and build in release mode
COPY . .

# Build the tauri workspace (tauri crate) in release mode
RUN cargo build --manifest-path 'Flying Carpet/src-tauri/Cargo.toml' --release


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebView TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3-0 ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the built binary from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet

# Run the app
CMD ["FlyingCarpet"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the repository and build in release mode (workspace build)
COPY . .

# Build all workspace crates in release mode
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Copy all release artifacts for runtime
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/ /usr/local/bin/

# Create a small wrapper to pick the correct binary if the name differs
RUN printf '#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nfor b in FlyingCarpet flyingcarpet FlyingCarpetApp; do\n  if [ -x "/usr/local/bin/$b" ]; then\n    exec "/usr/local/bin/$b" "$@";\n  fi;\ndone\n' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet

# Runtime image
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebView TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the built binaries from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/ /usr/local/bin/
COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet

# Run the wrapper script which will launch the actual binary
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \

# Copy the repository and build in release mode (workspace build)

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the repository and build in release mode
COPY . .

# Build all workspace crates in release mode
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Copy the tauri binary (best-effort, handle multiple possible names)
RUN set -e; \
    if [ -f /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet ]; then \
        cp /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet; \
    elif [ -f /workspace/target/release/flyingcarpet ]; then \
        cp /workspace/target/release/flyingcarpet /usr/local/bin/flyingcarpet; \
    fi; \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet 2>/dev/null || true; \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/flyingcarpet 2>/dev/null || true


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebView TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates libssl3 && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the released binaries from builder
COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet
COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin/flyingcarpet /usr/local/bin/flyingcarpet

# Wrapper script to execute the correct binary from the release artifacts
RUN printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/sh' 'set -e' 'BIN1="/usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet"' 'BIN2="/usr/local/bin/flyingcarpet"' 'if [ -x "$BIN1" ]; then' '  exec "$BIN1" "$@"' 'fi' 'if [ -x "$BIN2" ]; then' '  exec "$BIN2" "$@"' 'fi' 'echo "No executable found" >&2' 'exit 1' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh

# Run the wrapper script which will launch the actual binary
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FlyingCarpet (tauri) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the repository and build in release mode (workspace build)
COPY . .

# Build all workspace crates in release mode
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Expose the release artifacts for runtime
# Copy all release binaries from the builder to a known path in the image
# so the runtime stage can pick them up
COPY --from=builder /workspace/target/release/ /target-release/


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebView TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the release artifacts from the builder
COPY --from=builder /target-release/ /usr/local/bin/

# Wrapper script to execute the correct binary from the release artifacts
RUN printf '#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nfor f in /usr/local/bin/*; do\n  if [ -x "$f" ]; then\n    exec "$f" "$@"\n  fi\ndone\necho "No executable found" >&2\nexit 1\n' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh

# Run the wrapper script which will launch the actual binary
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh"]
"}

- Exact error message and exit code

- Failing command/step
  - Failing step: runtime stage COPY from the builder
  - Command in Dockerfile: COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet
  - Dockerfile reference: Dockerfile:30

- Missing packages or files mentioned
  - The runtime COPY fails because the source file does not exist in the builder stage:
    - Expected source: /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet
    - Actual issue: not found (the builder did not produce/copy FlyingCarpet to /usr/local/bin within the builder stage)
  - Context note: The builder build produced a binary at /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet (as seen in builder steps), but the subsequent COPY uses the /usr/local/bin path, and the binary was not present there.

- Version mismatch info
  - Rust workspace resolver warning:
    - "warning: virtual workspace defaulting to `resolver = \"1\"` despite one or more workspace members being on edition 2021 which implies `resolver = \"2\"`"
    - Suggests a resolver version mismatch for the Rust workspace; guidance given in log: set workspace.resolver = "1" or "2" in Cargo.toml to align with edition.
  - Base images observed:
    - Builder uses rust:1.94.0-bookworm
    - Runtime uses debian:bookworm-slim
  - Noted that these are newer/dependency-heavy install paths (apt-get steps in runtime show extensive package installs), but the immediate failure is the missing binary, not a missing package.

- What happened (condensed)
  - Builder steps completed and successfully built the release artifacts.
  - Step to copy the binary into the final image attempted, but the expected binary was not found at /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet (even though a copy step to /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet exists during build).
  - As a result, the final COPY from-builder to runtime fails with a not-found error.

- Quick remediation ideas
  - Verify that the cargo build actually produced FlyingCarpet at the expected path and that the binary is being placed into /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet in the builder stage (or adjust the COPY source path accordingly).
  - If the binary is indeed at /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet, change the final COPY to copy from /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet to the runtime image, or ensure a preceding step moves it to /usr/local/bin in the builder stage.
  - Address the Cargo workspace resolver mismatch by aligning workspace.resolver to "1" or "2" in the root Cargo.toml as appropriate for edition 2021 members.

- Note on logs
  - The large apt-get install churn in runtime is not the root cause; the root cause is the missing FlyingCarpet binary during the final multi-stage COPY.

  - Runtime: debian:bookworm-slim
- Noted that the failing cause is the missing binary, not the apt-get package churn in runtime

What happened (condensed)
- Builder steps completed and released artifacts; final COPY failed because /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet was not present in the builder stage

Quick remediation ideas
- Verify cargo build produced FlyingCarpet at the expected path and that the binary is placed into /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet in the builder stage (or adjust the COPY source path)
- If the binary is at /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet, modify the final COPY to /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet -> /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet, or add a step to move it to /usr/local/bin in the builder stage
- Align Cargo workspace resolver by setting workspace.resolver = "1" or "2" in the root Cargo.toml to match edition 2021 members
- The runtime apt-get churn is not the root cause; fix the binary path first

verify_build_details.review_concerns: ["The build stage copies the released binary with 'cp ... 2>/dev/null || true', which swallows errors and could mask a failed build; the subsequent COPY will fail if the binary isn't present, but this pattern is brittle.", "Running a TAURI desktop app in a headless container may not exercise the UI; smoke tests should account for potential headless execution or GUI forwarding requirements."]

  - Error: "ERROR: failed to calculate checksum of ref dm0crafjnw8l43ifl6uhde0ck::palv5c3jgj2nvni3cauw5yos0: \"/usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet\": not found"

  - Command in Dockerfile: COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet
  - Dockerfile reference: Dockerfile:30

- Missing packages or files mentioned

    - Expected source: /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet
    - Actual issue: not found (the builder did not produce/copy FlyingCarpet to /usr/local/bin within the builder stage)
  - Context note: The builder build produced a binary at /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet (as seen in builder steps), but the subsequent COPY uses the /usr/local/bin path, and the binary was not present there.

- Version mismatch info
  - Rust workspace resolver warning:
    - "warning: virtual workspace defaulting to `resolver = \"1\"` despite one or more workspace members being on edition 2021 which implies `resolver = \"2\"`"
    - Suggests a resolver version mismatch for the Rust workspace; guidance given in log: set workspace.resolver = "1" or "2" in Cargo.toml to align with edition.
  - Base images observed:

    - Runtime uses debian:bookworm-slim
  - Noted that these are newer/dependency-heavy install paths (apt-get steps in runtime show extensive package installs), but the immediate failure is the missing binary, not a missing package.

- What happened (condensed)

  - Step to copy the binary into the final image attempted, but the expected binary was not found at /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet (even though a copy step to /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet exists during build).

- Quick remediation ideas
  - Verify that the cargo build actually produced FlyingCarpet at the expected path and that the binary is being placed into /usr/local/bin/FlyingCarpet in the builder stage (or adjust the COPY source path accordingly).
  - If the binary is indeed at /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet, change the final COPY to copy from /workspace/target/release/FlyingCarpet to the runtime image, or ensure a preceding step moves it to /usr/local/bin in the builder stage.
  - Address the Cargo workspace resolver mismatch by aligning workspace.resolver to "1" or "2" in the root Cargo.toml as appropriate for edition 2021 members.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage build for FlyingCarpet (TAURI) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy source and build the workspace in release mode
COPY . .
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Collect release artifacts into a known directory
RUN mkdir -p /workspace/release
RUN sh -lc 'for f in /workspace/target/release/*; do if [ -x "$f" ]; then cp "$f" "/workspace/release/"; fi; done'


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebKit TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates libssl3 && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the release artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/release /workspace/release

# Wrapper script to execute the released binary
RUN printf '#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nBIN_DIR="/workspace/release"\nfor f in "$BIN_DIR"/*; do\n  if [ -x "$f" ]; then\n    exec "$f" "$@"\n  fi\ndone\nexit 1\n' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh

# Run the app (wrapper will pick the executable in the release dir)
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh"]
"}

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy source and build the workspace in release mode
COPY . .
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Collect release artifacts into a known directory
RUN mkdir -p /workspace/release
RUN sh -lc 'for f in /workspace/target/release/*; do if [ -x "$f" ]; then cp "$f" "/workspace/release/"; fi; done'


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebKit TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates libssl3 && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the release artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/release /workspace/release

# Wrapper script to execute the released binary
RUN printf '#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nBIN_DIR="/workspace/release"\nfor f in "$BIN_DIR"/*; do\n  if [ -x "$f" ]; then\n    exec "$f" "$@"\n  fi\ndone\nexit 1\n' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh

# Run the app (wrapper will pick the executable in the release dir)
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh"]
"}

# Multi-stage build for FlyingCarpet (TAURI) app

FROM rust:1.94.0-bookworm AS builder

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates build-essential pkg-config \
    libssl-dev nodejs npm libgtk-3-dev libsoup-3.0-dev libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy source and build the workspace in release mode
COPY . .
RUN cargo build --workspace --release

# Collect release artifacts into a known directory
RUN mkdir -p /workspace/release
RUN sh -lc 'for f in /workspace/target/release/*; do if [ -x "$f" ]; then cp "$f" "/workspace/release/"; fi; done'


FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS runtime

# Runtime dependencies for a GTK/WebKit TAURI app
RUN apt-get update -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0 libsoup-3.0-0 ca-certificates libssl3 && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy the release artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /workspace/release /workspace/release

# Wrapper script to execute the released binary
RUN printf '#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nBIN_DIR="/workspace/release"\nfor f in "$BIN_DIR"/*; do\n  if [ -x "$f" ]; then\n    exec "$f" "$@"\n  fi\ndone\nexit 1\n' > /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh && \
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh

# Run the app (wrapper will pick the executable in the release dir)
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/run-flyingcarpet.sh"]
